---
title: "Daniela Castro-Camilo"
page-layout: full
toc: false
title-block-banner: false
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

::: {.homepage}

::: {.hero-home}
::: {.hero-inner}
::: {.hero-copy}

# Daniela Castro-Camilo

::: {.hero-role}
Senior Lecturer in Statistics  
University of Glasgow
:::

::: {.hero-line}
:::

I develop statistical methods to understand, predict and attribute environmental and disaster-related extremes. My work lies at the interface of extreme value theory, spatial and spatio-temporal statistics, Bayesian inference and causal inference, with applications in the natural environment and critical infrastructure.

A recurring theme in my work is the development of practical, user-friendly methods that better capture rare and impactful events, rather than treating them as outliers to be smoothed away.

<!-- ::: {.hero-actions} -->
<!-- [{{< fa file-lines >}} View CV](cv.pdf){.pill .pill-primary} -->
<!-- [{{< fa envelope >}} Contact](mailto:Daniela.CastroCamilo@glasgow.ac.uk){.pill .pill-secondary} -->
<!-- ::: -->

:::

::: {.hero-photo-wrap}
![](assets/daniela-profile.png){.hero-photo fig-alt="Daniela Castro-Camilo"}
:::
:::
:::

::: {.glance-section}

## Research at a glance

::: {.title-rule}
:::

::: {.glance-grid}
::: {.glance-card}
<span class="research-icon">≋</span>

<div class="glance-title">Causality for extremes</div>

Extreme-event attribution and causal inference
:::

::: {.glance-card}
<span class="research-icon">◎</span>

<div class="glance-title">Spatial extremes</div>

Dependence modelling in space and time
:::

::: {.glance-card}
<span class="research-icon">⌬</span>
<div class="glance-title">INLA and SPDE models</div>
Fast Bayesian inference for spatial models
:::

::: {.glance-card}
<span class="research-icon">△</span>
<div class="glance-title">Landslide modelling</div>
Hazard assessment and susceptibility modelling
:::

::: {.glance-card}
<span class="research-icon">☘</span>
<div class="glance-title">Extremes in environment & ecology</div>
Statistical methods for environmental and ecological extremes

:::

::: {.glance-card}
<span class="research-icon">ϟ</span>
<div class="glance-title">Energy and infrastructure</div>
Weather-driven risk for energy systems and critical infrastructure
:::
:::

[Explore all research areas →](research.qmd){.explore-button}

:::

<!-- ::: {.contact-strip} -->
<!-- ::: {.contact-item} -->
<!-- <span class="contact-icon">🎓</span> -->
<!-- <span>Senior Lecturer in Statistics<br>University of Glasgow</span> -->
<!-- ::: -->

<!-- ::: {.contact-item} -->
<!-- <span class="contact-icon">📍</span> -->
<!-- <span>James Watt South Building<br>Glasgow, UK</span> -->
<!-- ::: -->

<!-- ::: {.contact-item} -->
<!-- <span class="contact-icon">✉</span> -->
<!-- <span>[daniela.castro-camilo@glasgow.ac.uk](mailto:Daniela.CastroCamilo@glasgow.ac.uk)</span> -->
<!-- ::: -->

<!-- ::: {.contact-item} -->
<!-- <span class="orcid-dot">iD</span> -->
<!-- <span>[ORCID: 0000-0002-7536-4613](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-7536-4613)</span> -->
<!-- ::: -->
<!-- ::: -->

:::
